title: Can You Inject Near Or Into A Birth Mark？ | Dr Tim's Tips

excerpt: So can you treat near or inject into a birthmark or for that matter any skin lesion [Music] so it will be an absolute no i think most people get this to inject anything that was cancerous because that could cause spread of cancer's cancer cells so we would never go anywhere near …

segments.text: so can you treat near or inject into a birthmark or for that matter any skin lesion [Music] so it will be an absolute no i think most people get this to inject anything that was cancerous because that could cause spread of cancer's cancer cells so we would never go anywhere near even within the perimeter if you find out someone had a melanoma that they were going to have cut out in a few weeks time i think it's a rare situation but you wouldn't go anywhere near that if it's just a normal kind of a birthmark or some other skin lesion like a mole you're obviously going to do everything you can to avoid injecting it i don't think there would be any specific risk with injecting it but i wouldn't want to be trying to find out i think in most cases you can find a way of not going near it now if you if you're treating near something it's a very vascular region uh lesion like a strawberry never for example you can have a way more chance of bruising bleeding and you just want to do everything you can in your power to stay out of that area but i don't i

stay out of that area but i don't think other than trauma you're likely to cause much if you were to accidentally you know pierce a mole for example it's tissue that will recover with time you just want to try and keep things simple by not going near it but it's unlikely to cause any obvious problems
